title: Peppery Juicy Argentinian Hazelnuts Burger
description: A vibrant, peppery vegan burger packed with hazelnuts, quinoa, and black beans, finished under a broiler for a crisp, juicy bite—perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: hazelnuts, toasted
ingredients.notes: toast in dry skillet until fragrant

instructions: Toast hazelnuts in a dry skillet over medium heat until fragrant, about 5 minutes, then set aside.

In a food processor, combine the cooked black beans, quinoa, toasted hazelnuts, chopped onion, chickpea flour, flax egg, olive oil, paprika, cumin, pepper, and a pinch of salt. Pulse until the mixture holds together but remains slightly chunky.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and shape into four equal patties, pressing gently to flatten.
Preheat the oven broiler and a cast‑iron skillet over medium‑high heat. Drizzle a tiny bit of oil into the skillet.
Place the patties in the skillet and broil each side for 3–4 minutes, or until the edges are crisp and juices begin to bubble—about 6–8 minutes total.
While the patties cook, place the buns on a baking sheet and broil for 1–2 minutes, watching closely, until lightly browned.

To assemble, spread a spoonful of chimichurri on each bun, place a patty on the bottom half, top with a lettuce leaf and tomato slice if desired, add more chimichurri, then cap with the top bun. Serve hot.
